name: Utah vs Idaho Workers' Comp for Latent Respiratory Diseases

mainEntity.name: Is there a difference between Utah and Idaho workers' comp for latent respiratory diseases like asbestosis?
mainEntity.text: Is there a difference between Utah and Idaho workers' comp for latent respiratory diseases like asbestosis?

acceptedAnswer.text: Utah uses a 6-year exposure/3-year discovery rule with medical panel reviews (Utah Code §34A-2-417); Idaho applies a 1-year discovery rule for occupational diseases (Idaho Code §72-702) with stricter causation proof.

suggestedAnswer.text: Utah offers more flexibility for latent claims like asbestosis from Millcreek insulation work, allowing extensions via commission approval. Idaho requires filing within 1 year of diagnosis and diagnosis. Both cap TTD at 72-104 weeks but differ in PPI calculations. Cross-state commuters need jurisdiction analysis; dual filings possible but complex.
